title: TC-VL50-CC-001: Verify 'Can't Be Updated' message appears for Voyager Legend 50 Charge Case on outdated firmware
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app on the phone.
Ensure Bluetooth is enabled and the Voyager Legend 50 headset is paired and connected.
Tap the connected [Voyager Legend 50] device name on the home screen.
Navigate to: Settings → Software.
Observe the Software screen content for the headset firmware.
Scroll down to the 'Charge Case' section under Device Components.
Verify the Charge Case firmware version is displayed.
Check if an 'Update' button is present next to the Charge Case firmware version.
If no 'Update' button is present, verify that a 'Can't be updated' message (or equivalent greyed-out / informational text) is displayed instead.
Take a screenshot of the Software screen for documentation.
expected: The Software screen for Voyager Legend 50 shows the Charge Case firmware version. Instead of an 'Update' button, the app displays a 'Can't be updated' message (or equivalent text indicating the charge case firmware cannot be updated via the app), confirming the error/negative-path UI state for the Legend 50 Charge Case.

title: TC-VL50-CC-002: Verify Voyager Legend 50 Charge Case firmware update button is absent when app cannot update
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app.
Connect to a Voyager Legend 50 headset with a Charge Case that is on an outdated firmware version (pre-condition: firmware version < latest available).
Tap the [Voyager Legend 50] device tile.
Navigate to: Settings → Software.
Locate the 'Charge Case' section in the device components list.
Verify the displayed firmware version string for the Charge Case.
Check for the presence of any interactive button (e.g., 'Update', 'Update Now', 'Update All').
If no update button exists, verify the informational message displayed (e.g., 'Can't be updated', 'Firmware update not available for this component', or similar).
Verify the message is non-interactive (tapping does nothing).
Navigate back to the Device screen, then re-enter Settings → Software to confirm the message persists.
expected: The Voyager Legend 50 Charge Case shows its firmware version but no 'Update' button is present. A 'Can't be updated' informational message is displayed instead. The message is non-interactive and persists across screen re-navigation.

title: TC-VL50-CC-003: Verify Voyager Legend 50 headset firmware update is still available when Charge Case shows 'Can't be updated'
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app with a Voyager Legend 50 headset connected (headset on outdated firmware, Charge Case also on outdated firmware).
Navigate to: Settings → Software.
Locate the 'Headset' (or 'Earpiece') section in the device components list.
Verify that an 'Update' button IS present for the headset firmware.
Locate the 'Charge Case' section below the headset.
Verify that the Charge Case shows 'Can't be updated' message (no update button).
Tap the 'Update' button for the headset firmware.
Confirm the firmware update process starts for the headset only.
After headset update completes, return to Settings → Software.
Verify the Charge Case still shows 'Can't be updated' message.
expected: The headset firmware update button is available and functional even when the Charge Case shows 'Can't be updated'. The Charge Case message persists after headset firmware update completes. The two components are treated independently in the UI.

title: TC-VL50-CC-004: Verify 'Can't be updated' message persists across app lifecycle for Voyager Legend 50 Charge Case
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app and connect to Voyager Legend 50 headset.
Navigate to: Settings → Software.
Confirm the Charge Case section shows 'Can't be updated' message (no update button).
Press the Home button to background the app.
Wait 10 seconds, then reopen the app from the recent apps list.
Navigate back to: Settings → Software.
Verify the Charge Case still shows 'Can't be updated' message.
Fully kill the app (swipe away from recent apps).
Reopen Poly Studio Mobile app.
Wait for the Voyager Legend 50 to reconnect automatically (or tap to connect).
Navigate to: Settings → Software.
Verify the Charge Case still shows 'Can't be updated' message.
expected: The 'Can't be updated' message for the Voyager Legend 50 Charge Case persists across app backgrounding (home button) and full app restart (force kill and relaunch), confirming the UI state is stable across app lifecycle transitions.

title: TC-VL50-CC-005: Verify 'Can't be updated' message persists across Bluetooth disconnect/reconnect for Voyager Legend 50 Charge Case
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app and connect to Voyager Legend 50 headset.
Navigate to: Settings → Software.
Confirm the Charge Case section shows 'Can't be updated' message.
Turn off Bluetooth on the phone.
Wait 5 seconds.
Turn Bluetooth back on.
Wait for the Voyager Legend 50 to automatically reconnect.
Open Poly Studio Mobile app (if not already open).
Navigate to: Settings → Software.
Verify the Charge Case section still shows 'Can't be updated' message.
Manually disconnect the headset from the phone's Bluetooth settings.
Re-pair and reconnect the Voyager Legend 50 headset.
Open Poly Studio Mobile app.
Navigate to: Settings → Software.
Verify the Charge Case section still shows 'Can't be updated' message.
expected: The 'Can't be updated' message for the Voyager Legend 50 Charge Case persists across Bluetooth toggle (off/on) and full disconnect/reconnect cycle, confirming the UI state is stable across Bluetooth state transitions.

title: TC-VL50-CC-006: Verify Voyager Legend 50 Charge Case shows correct UI (no false 'Update' button) on latest firmware
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app and connect to a Voyager Legend 50 headset whose Charge Case is already on the latest available firmware.
Navigate to: Settings → Software.
Locate the 'Charge Case' section.
Verify the firmware version displayed matches the latest known version.
Verify that NO 'Update' button is displayed (since firmware is already current).
Verify that NO 'Can't be updated' error message is displayed (since this is the expected up-to-date state, not an error).
Verify the Charge Case status shows as 'Up to date' or equivalent positive confirmation text.
Take a screenshot for comparison with the outdated firmware state.
expected: When the Voyager Legend 50 Charge Case is on the latest firmware, the Software screen shows 'Up to date' status (or equivalent) for the Charge Case. No 'Update' button and no 'Can't be updated' error message are displayed. The UI correctly distinguishes between 'up to date' and 'can't be updated' states.

title: TC-VL50-CC-007: Verify Voyager Legend 50 Charge Case 'Can't be updated' message is not shown for Voyager Free 60+ Charge Case
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app and connect to a Voyager Free 60+ headset (with touchscreen charge case).
Navigate to: Settings → Software.
Locate the 'Charge Case' section.
Verify that the Voyager Free 60+ Charge Case shows either an 'Update' button (if outdated) or 'Up to date' status.
Verify that NO 'Can't be updated' message is displayed for the Voyager Free 60+ Charge Case.
Disconnect the Voyager Free 60+.
Connect the Voyager Legend 50 headset.
Navigate to: Settings → Software.
Verify the Voyager Legend 50 Charge Case DOES show 'Can't be updated' message.
This confirms the message is device-specific and not a global app bug.
expected: The 'Can't be updated' message is specific to the Voyager Legend 50 Charge Case and does NOT appear for the Voyager Free 60+ Charge Case. This confirms the UI correctly differentiates between charge case architectures (non-touchscreen Legend 50 vs. smart touchscreen Free 60+).
